questions.question: Consider the following statements: 1. In the First Round Table Conference, Dr. Ambedkar demanded separate electorates for the depressed classes.2. In the Poona Act, special provisions for representation of the depressed people in the local bodies and civil services were made.3. The Indian National Congress did not take part in the Third Round Table Conference.Which of the statements is/are correct? (UPSC Prelims 2005)
questions.explanation: First Round Table Conference was held from Nov, 1930 – Jan 1931. Second Round Table Conference was held in Dec 1931 in London. Third Round Table Conference was held from Nov 1932 – Dec 1932. Indian National Congress did not participate in first and third RTC. Poona Pact was signed by B.R. Ambedkar in September, 1932. The pact abandoned separate electorates for the depressed classes. But the seats reserved for the depressed classes were increased in provincial as well as Central legislatures.

questions.question: Who said that “Buxar finally riveted the shackles of Company’s rule upon Bengal”?
questions.explanation: The battle of Buxar was fought in the year 1764. Sir Ramsay Muir opined that “Buxar finally riveted the shackles of Company’s rule upon Bengal”.

questions.question: Lord Cornwallis sent an instruction to who of the following English resident of Poona to approach Peshwas to fight against Tipu Sultan?
questions.explanation: Lord Cornwallis sent an order to Malet (English resident of Poona) to approach Peshwas to fight against Tipu Sultan. Englished offered some attractive terms to Marathas. In the year 1790, Peshwa signed a treaty with the English.

questions.question: Who of the following introduced the Ryotwari system?
questions.explanation: Thomas Munro (Governor of Madras) introduced the Ryotwari system in the year 1820. It was started in southern India (Except some areas of Madras Presidency) and South Western India. The revenue had to be paid by the tenants directly to the government. The rate of revenue was very high and used to change after 30 years.

questions.question: Which among the following events took place during the reign of Lord Amherst?1. First Anglo Burmese War2. Capture of Bharatpur3. Mutiny at BarrackporeChoose the correct option from the codes given below :
questions.explanation: First Anglo Burmese War took place in the year 1824-26. The capture of Bharatpur took place in 1826. Mutiny at Barrackpore started in 1824.

questions.question: Who of the following blamed the Doctrine of Lapse of Lord Dalhousie for being a cause of the great revolt of 1857?
questions.explanation: The doctrine of Lapse created a fearful environment among the ruler of the states of India and created dissatisfaction among the Hindus. Historian Smith blamed it for being a major cause of the great revolt of 1857.

questions.question: The Armies of the Indian States was renamed as the Imperial Service Troops during tenure of which of the following Viceroys?
questions.explanation: During the viceroyship of Lord Lansdowne Indian Council Act of 1892 passed. New Factory Law of 1891 passed, which modified the old law which was passed during the time of Lord Ripon. The Armies of the Indian States was renamed as the Imperial Service Troops. The downfall of the Indian economy was noticed due to the devaluation of Silver.

questions.question: The Indian Universities Act was passed in which year?
questions.explanation: The Indian Universities Act was passed in the year 1904 during the time of Lord Curzon. It was established by the recommendation of Releigh Commission.

questions.question: Which of the following Act provided for the establishment of a Public service commission?
questions.explanation: Setting up of Public Service Commission was provided in the Government of India Act of 1919. Central Public service commission was established in the year 1926 by the recommendation of Lee commission.

questions.question: Who among the following viceroys of India appointed Aitchison Committee?
questions.explanation: Aitchison Committee was appointed by Lord Dufferin in the year 1886. The main objective of the committee was to make a plan which might be ensured to have the fundamental components of certainty and to do full equity to the case of locals of India to higher and increasingly broad work in the Public Service.
